title: Bump golang to 1.17
body:
Go 1.17 compiler got faster

Signed-off-by: Pascal Bourdier <pascal.bourdier@gmail.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body: Wouldn't this mean that if someone runs `make vendor-in-container` and commit the changes they would be not pass CI because CI compares that against make vendor with go 1.16?

> Wouldn't this mean that if someone runs `make vendor-in-container` and commit the changes they would be not pass CI because CI compares that against make vendor with go 1.16?

I run go 1.18 beta and don't have any issues.  But I see wisdom in your concern.  It's probably a good idea to have vendor-in-container and the consistency check to use the _same_ container image. @cevich WDYT?

I am OK to merge this PR as is and do the remainder in a separate one.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *PascalBourdier*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> But I see wisdom in your concern. It's probably a good idea to have vendor-in-container and the consistency check to use the same container image. @cevich WDYT?

This is possible to do.  No new container image is needed since we already build one (ref `quay.io/libpod/fedora_podman:$IMAGE_SUFFIX`).  This can be guaranteed to always be in-sync with CI automation. by following the example in skopeo's Makefile (ref. `SKOPEO_CIDEV_CONTAINER_FQIN`) + a `hack/get_fqin.sh` script.  It's slightly convoluted how it works, since it involves parsing of `.cirrus.yml`.  But it's been in use/successful in skopeo for quite a long while.

> I am OK to merge this PR as is and do the remainder in a separate one.

If it helps...for similar needs that require coordination with new images, I can always rebase an image-update PR onto other PRs so they all get merged together.
